title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Global aluminum prices remained flat at $3,461.28/Ton and ¥23,822/Ton in China, with a 0.00% daily change amid market consolidation. Iranian missile strikes damaged key smelters like Emirates Global Aluminium and Alba, while the Strait of Hormuz closure disrupted Gulf exports, causing LME inventories to drop sharply. Analysts forecast a 570,000-tonne global deficit, signaling potential price upside.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices fell to 3,446 USD/T on April 9, 2026, down 0.92% from the prior day, though up 1.56% over the past month and 45.46% year-over-year. UK aluminum futures approached $3,500 per tonne near four-year highs amid investor caution before President Trump's Iran peace deal deadline. Forecasts predict prices at 3,492.52 USD/T by quarter-end and 3,657.58 in 12 months.

title: AMAG and Pilatus Sign Multi-Year Agreement for Aluminum Products in Aircraft
summary: AMAG and Pilatus have entered into a multi-year agreement for aluminum products in aircraft applications. This partnership represents continued innovation in aerospace aluminum solutions.

title: EGA and Alba Damaged In Recent Attacks from Iran
summary: Major aluminum producers EGA and Alba sustained damage from recent attacks originating from Iran. This incident impacts global aluminum supply and production capacity.

title: GARMCO Upgrades Hot Rolling Mill for Enhanced Operational Efficiency
summary: Gulf Aluminium Rolling Mill B.S.C. (GARMCO) completed an upgrade of its 1-stand hot rolling mill through SMS group, enhancing operational efficiency and reliability to meet future demands in aluminum processing.[3]

title: Brimstone Reveals 'Fourth Great Refinery' Vision Extending Beyond Carbon-Free Cement
summary: Brimstone, a U.S. industrial technology innovator, unveiled its Rock Refinery® process roadmap that now targets aluminum, steel, titanium, magnesium, and critical minerals alongside its pioneering carbon-free cement technology.[3]

title: Sortera Technologies Plans Second Advanced Scrap Processing Facility
summary: Sortera Technologies plans to build a second scrap processing facility in Lebanon, Tennessee, using artificial intelligence and advanced sensors to sort mixed aluminum scrap into specific alloys.[3]

title: Scientists Create RidgeAlloy: New Aluminum Alloy from Contaminated Car-Body Scrap
summary: Oak Ridge National Laboratory scientists developed RidgeAlloy, a new aluminum alloy capable of converting contaminated car-body scrap into usable material, advancing aluminum recycling and sustainability.[9]

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,080 CNY/T on April 10, 2026, up 0.29% from the previous day, amid a temporary reopening of the Strait of Hormuz easing supply disruption concerns following a US-Iran ceasefire agreement. Steel rebar futures held below CNY 3,090 per ton at one-month lows. Over the past month, prices have fallen 1.44% despite being 0.65% higher year-to-date.

title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
summary: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement led to sharp drops in European gas and Brent crude prices, potentially relieving steel production costs, though slab and billet prices in Asia remain elevated due to prior energy and freight cost surges. Traders note no immediate impact on steel prices today, but a stronger euro affects import pricing. Risks persist if the Strait of Hormuz ceasefire is interrupted, deterring Gulf cargoes.

title: Plate market fears tightening supplies, escalating freight costs
summary: US plate market participants report concerns over escalating fuel and freight expenses, tariff complications, long lead times, delivery delays, and shrinking spot availability from domestic mills. Consolidated end-market demand adds pressure. Algoma Steel announced immediate plate price increases of US$60/ton plus a potential fuel surcharge.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel traded flat at 1,082.02 USD/T on April 9, 2026, after increasing to 996.00 USD/T and later 1,075.00 USD/T, marking the highest since January 2024. Over the past month, prices rose 3.25%, up 16.35% year-over-year. The past four weeks saw a 2.8-4.99% gain.

title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
summary: U.S. steel manufacturers are planning over $6 billion in capital-spending projects set to begin in Q2 2026, with major projects in Texas and Louisiana. Nippon Steel-owned United States Steel is adding a premium thread line at its Fairfield, Alabama facility and planning upgrades at its Mon Valley Works plant in Pennsylvania. Hyundai Steel is advancing its 2.7 million ton-per-year steel mill project in Modeste, Louisiana using direct-reduction technology.

title: Industry Data - Week Ending April 4, 2026
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,831,000 net tons in the week ending April 4, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 79.1 percent, representing an 8.2 percent increase from the same period last year. Year-to-date production through April 4, 2026 was 24,028,000 net tons at a 77.4 percent utilization rate, up 5.2 percent from the prior year.

title: Plate Market Fears Tightening Supplies, Escalating Freight Costs
summary: Plate market participants report concerns about escalating fuel and freight expenses, tariff complications, long lead times, and shrinking availability of spot tons from domestic mills. The market is experiencing delivery delays and supply tightness across the sector.

title: GSCC: Launches Two-Year Review of Steel Climate Standard, Certifies Duferco's CASEI and SBET
summary: The Global Steel Climate Council (GSCC) announced it will begin a two-year review of the Steel Climate Standard and its supporting technical guidance. The council also certified Duferco Travi e Profilati S.p.A.'s Corporate Average Steel Emissions Intensity (CASEI) and Science-Based Emissions Target (SBET).

title: Calderys Launches World's Largest Single-Site Greenfield Facility for Refractories, Steel Casting Fluxes
summary: Calderys officially launched CAPES, the world's largest single-site greenfield facility for refractories and steel casting fluxes, located in Odisha. This facility represents a significant advancement in steel production infrastructure.

title: Mesabi Metallics Secures US$150 Million for American DR-Grade Iron Ore Mine and Pellet Plant
summary: Mesabi Metallics Co. LLC and Essar Group secured US$150 million in financing from Macquarie Group for a direct reduction (DR)–grade iron ore mine and pellet plant in Nashwauk, Minnesota, scheduled to start operations in Q3 2026. This investment supports sustainable steelmaking through direct reduction technology.

title: Fives Group Signs Contract with Hyundai Steel for Advanced Coil Finishing Lines
summary: Fives Group announced a contract with Hyundai Steel to design and supply advanced coil finishing lines for automotive steel production, supporting innovation in steel manufacturing technology.

title: Free SAIL's Bokaro Steel Mill to Start Hydrogen Injection at Blast Furnace to Cut Carbon Emissions
summary: Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL) will begin hydrogen injection at its Bokaro steel mill's blast furnace to reduce carbon emissions, representing a key innovation in decarbonizing steel production.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group aligned with illicit mineral activities in Congo and related mining firms. This action targets disruptions in the supply chain for critical minerals including copper. The sanctions aim to curb funding for armed conflicts through mineral trade.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology has produced its first copper at the Johnson Camp mine in Arizona, achieving the lowest mine-to-metal carbon footprint in the U.S. at 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper. The technology enables cleaner, faster production with up to 80% less water usage and 60% lower emissions than traditional methods, while extending mine life by processing waste ores. It reached industrial scale in just 18 months, far quicker than the industry average.

title: CMOC Joins the International Copper Association to Advance Sustainable Industry Development
summary: CMOC Group Limited has joined the International Copper Association as its first Chinese member to promote sustainable development in the copper industry. This membership supports advancements in market, policy, mining, energy, and sustainability practices. It highlights copper's evolving role beyond industrial material toward broader sustainability goals.

title: Cogne Acciai Speciali SpA Announces Its Commitment to Sustainability with ResponsibleSteel™ Certification
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali has embarked on the ResponsibleSteel™ certification process for its Aosta site, a global standard promoting sustainability across the steel production value chain. This decision formalizes the company's commitment to environmental, social, and transparency standards as the steel industry faces increasing pressure to meet environmental and social challenges.

title: Cogne Acciai Speciali: 94% of the energy used comes from renewable sources and 93% of employees have permanent contracts
summary: On World Environment Day, Cogne Acciai Speciali released its first Group Sustainability Report, highlighting that over 94% of electricity used by the Group came from certified renewable sources by the end of 2024. The report demonstrates the company's progress toward a more responsible and transparent industrial model and its commitment to fighting climate change.
